projectDescription: A Lakehouse management system built on open data lake formats (Iceberg, Hudi, Paimon, Mixed) that provides self-optimizing, catalog services, and unified management for Flink, Spark, and Trino compute engines.
keyFeatures: Self-optimizing: Continuous background file compaction, deduplication, and layout optimization with minor/major/full optimizing types
Multi-format support: Native Iceberg, Mixed-Iceberg, Mixed-Hive, and Paimon table formats
Unified catalog service: Centralized metadata management compatible with Hive Metastore and AWS Glue
Multi-engine integration: Full support for Flink, Spark, and Trino compute engines
Resource management: Optimizer groups with quota-based and balanced scheduling policies
Dashboard UI: Web-based management interface for catalogs, tables, and optimizers
REST API: OpenAPI-compliant HTTP API for programmatic access to AMS features
Plugin system: Extensible architecture for metric reporters, event listeners, and custom integrations
High availability: ZooKeeper-based leader election for production deployments
Kubernetes deployment: Helm charts for cloud-native Lakehouse management
publicApiSurface: REST API: /api/ams/v1/* — catalog, table, optimizer, and metrics endpoints
Thrift API: TableMetastore service on port 1260 (compute engine catalog integration)
Thrift API: OptimizingService on port 1261 (optimizer communication)
FormatCatalogFactory SPI — plug in custom table format support
SparkTableFormat SPI — custom Spark catalog integration
ProcessFactory SPI — custom optimizing process implementations
MetricReporter plugin — integrate with external monitoring systems (e.g., Prometheus)
AmoroTable Java API — programmatic table metadata access
OpenAPI SDK — generated client library from openapi.yaml spec
